findings.description: The HTML duplicates the entire content of the page. After presenting a formatted version of the page, it immediately appends an unformatted raw text dump of the same content.
findings.evidence: In the HTML rendering, the DEQ footer at the bottom of the formatted letter is immediately followed by a repeat of the header text ('ROY COOPER...', 'April 4, 2022') and the beginning of the letter body.

findings.description: The reading order is disrupted by the continuation of the duplicated text from the previous page.
findings.evidence: The HTML rendering for this section begins with the second half of the duplicated Page 1 text (bulleted list and footer) before starting the actual header and content for Page 2.

findings.description: The HTML duplicates the content of the page, appending a raw text version after the formatted section.
findings.evidence: The HTML rendering shows the complete page content including the signature and cc list, and then immediately shows the Page 3 header ('Daniel Blackman April 4, 2022 Page 3') again at the bottom, initiating a duplicate text block.

findings.description: The HTML presents content where the source PDF has a blank page.
findings.evidence: The PDF page is completely blank, but the HTML rendering contains the full raw text duplicate of the content from Page 3.
